description: Letter from Yeshuʿa ha-Melammed b. Avraham, in Minyat Zifta, to Eliyyahu the Judge, in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 1236 CE (Iyyar 4800+196 = 4996 AM, line 16). The writer reports that the income from the ‘quppa’ was used as a school fee for poor children. Goitein also makes note of the greeting for an old man in precarious health in a high position: "May God not wound Israel through him" (line 4), i.e., may God not wound Israel by causing him illness or death. (S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 2:105-6, 515, 544.) EMS. ASE.
tags: Eliyyahu b. Zekharia, quppa, illness: public figure, 13th c, education, dating tricks, 4800, Illness, illness letter 969-1517, Minyat Zifta
description_he: מכתב מישועה המלמד בן אברהם, מנית זפתא, לאליהו השופט, פוסטאט, 1236: הכנסות ה'קופה' שימשו כשכ"ל לילדים עניים. ברכה לזקן בריאות טובה בתפקיד גבוה: 'שלא יפצע אלוהים ישראל דרכו'.
